summary: Added a partial decompilation for the tomato item translation unit, bringing `src/melee/it/items/ittomato.c` to a reported 74.75% match. The PR implemented many small item state callbacks, added the `it_803F5740` item state table, left three functions non-matching in the PR report, adjusted `symbols.txt` scopes for internal item functions and local sdata constants, and moved `gm_80473A18` declaration into `gm_1601.h` as `extern UNK_T`. Review focused on project conventions: static/local symbol scope should be based on cross-translation-unit references, externs belong in headers, item state table sentinel values should be written as `-1`, empty void functions can be `{}`, and `GET_ITEM(gobj)`/combined declarations are preferred when codegen allows.
what_changed: Populated previously empty `src/melee/it/items/ittomato.c` with 246 lines of item code, including static forward declarations, an `ItemStateTable it_803F5740`, and implementations/stubs for tomato item functions from `it_802841B4` through `it_80284624`.

Implemented many matching/simple item callbacks: animation state transitions through `Item_80268E5C`, gravity/fall speed updates via `it_80272860`, collision/state handoffs via `it_8026E15C`, `it_8026D62C`, and `it_8026E8C4`, JObj flag clearing with `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll`, and a reference callback wrapper around `it_8026B894`.

Added partial/non-perfect implementations for larger functions such as `it_802841B4`, `it_802842A4`, and `it_80284324`, using M2C helper macros such as `M2C_FIELD` and `M2C_BITWISE` where types or structure layouts were not yet fully resolved.
Added `#include "gm/gm_1601.h"` in `ittomato.c` and declared `/* 473A18 */ extern UNK_T gm_80473A18;` in `src/melee/gm/gm_1601.h` instead of keeping an `extern M2C_UNK` directly in the C file.
Added `<placeholder.h>` include to `ittomato.h`, likely to support placeholder/unknown type macros used by exposed declarations or included context.

Changed numerous `ittomato` symbols in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` from `scope:global` to `scope:local` for functions implemented as `static` and renamed two local `.sdata2` constants from global-looking `it_804DC8B0`/`it_804DC8B4` to local labels `@176`/`@180`.

Preserved global scope in `symbols.txt` for externally exposed functions that remain in `ittomato.h`, including `it_802842A4`, `it_80284324`, `it_802844E4`, `it_8028454C`, `it_802845BC`, and `it_80284624`.
smart_moves: Kept non-matching work mergeable rather than blocking on full completion; the PR report explicitly identified remaining non-matching functions so future work can target them.
Converted internal callbacks to `static` and aligned `symbols.txt` to `scope:local`, reducing accidental public API surface for item state callbacks.
Moved the `gm_80473A18` extern into the owning game-mode header and used `UNK_T`, matching reviewer guidance and keeping M2C-specific unknownness abstracted behind the project macro.
Used existing item helper macros and APIs (`GET_ITEM`, `GET_JOBJ`, `Item_80268E5C`, `ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE`) for small callbacks instead of open-coding access patterns where possible.
Represented item state table first fields as `-1` rather than `0xFFFFFFFF`, following reviewer style feedback while preserving the same value.
Used empty function bodies for do-nothing void callbacks, e.g. `void it_802845EC(Item_GObj* gobj) {}`, instead of `return;` inside the body after review.
decomp_lessons: For static vs global scope, determine whether a symbol is referenced outside the translation unit; reviewers recommended full-project text search or ripgrep for the symbol name as a practical check.

When a function is internal-only, update both the C declaration to `static` and `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` to `scope:local`; the project accepts these scope fixes even when tooling support is imperfect.
Extern declarations should generally live in the relevant header, not ad hoc at the top of an unrelated C file.
`UNK_T` is preferred for shared unknown data declarations because it expands appropriately for normal builds and M2C contexts, whereas raw `M2C_UNK` in headers/C files is less portable stylistically.
For no-op callbacks, an empty body `{}` is acceptable and preferred over an explicit `return;` when the function returns void.

Combining declaration and initialization is stylistically preferred when it does not affect codegen; for item pointers, try `Item* item = GET_ITEM(gobj);` before falling back to direct `gobj->user_data` access.
For item state tables, use signed sentinel spelling `-1` when the field semantically represents the all-bits-set sentinel, rather than writing `0xFFFFFFFF`.

Address comments and sorted declaration blocks are project conventions but not always mandatory; reviewer mentioned using editor substitution plus `tools/sort-declarations.sh` to generate/sort address-commented declarations.
naming_conventions: Item file follows the address-derived symbol style `it_8028xxxx` for still-unnamed tomato functions.
Internal item callbacks are declared `static` in the C file and have matching `scope:local` entries in `symbols.txt`.
Externally referenced tomato functions remain declared in `src/melee/it/items/ittomato.h` and retain `scope:global` in `symbols.txt`.
Local unnamed `.sdata2` constants use local labels like `@176` and `@180` rather than global-looking `it_804DC8B0` names.
Unknown game-mode global declaration uses `extern UNK_T gm_80473A18` in `gm_1601.h`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used M2C compatibility macros `M2C_FIELD` and `M2C_BITWISE` to express unresolved struct-field accesses and bit reinterpretations while keeping partially matched code compiling.

Used stack-like temporary variable names such as `sp5C`, `sp58`, `sp54`, and register-derived names such as `temp_r5`, `var_r3`, and `temp_r30` in non-matching/lower-confidence functions, indicating m2c-derived or assembly-guided reconstruction.

Kept some imperfect functions implemented rather than `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`; reviewer noted there is no need to remove code just because it is not matching, since compilable code is better than a missing function body.
The PR report called out `it_802844E4` and `it_8028454C` as containing branch arrows despite 100% score, which is useful for future cleanup or control-flow review.

State table callbacks were reconstructed with repeated patterns: return-false physics callbacks, per-state animation changes, fall-speed application from `ItemAttr`, and collision transition helper calls.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: `ItemStateTable it_803F5740` should go at the top of `ittomato.c` as a project convention.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Use `void it_802845EC(Item_GObj* gobj) {}` instead of a body containing only `return;`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.topic: extern placement and unknown type macro
review_feedback.feedback: `gm_80473A18` belongs in `src/melee/gm/gm_1601.h`; use `UNK_T`, which expands to `void*` most of the time and to `M2C_UNK` when using M2C.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Declarations can usually be combined with initialization without affecting codegen; `Item* ip = gobj->user_data;` is preferred, and `Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj);` is preferred if it does not break the match.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: First fields in the item state table should be expressed as `-1` rather than `0xFFFFFFFF`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Address comments are optional if burdensome; reviewer shared a vim substitution and `tools/sort-declarations.sh` workflow for generating and sorting declaration comments.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Do not remove compilable nonmatching code just because it is not perfect; `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` only exists to satisfy clang when there is otherwise no function body.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Modifying symbol scopes is correct if the functions are static. Static-ness should be determined by whether the symbol is referenced outside the translation unit, using full-project text search/ripgrep if needed.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish or improve non-matching `ittomato` functions `it_802841B4`, `it_802842A4`, and `it_80284324`.
Search for later renames or struct typing of `gm_80473A18` and the `M2C_FIELD` access at offset `0x90`.
Search for follow-up work replacing `M2C_FIELD` accesses in `ittomato.c` with named special-attribute or item-var struct fields.
Check whether `it_802844E4` and `it_8028454C` branch-arrow notes were later resolved despite their reported 100% score.
Compare other item decomp PRs for the same `ItemStateTable` pattern using `-1`, static callbacks, and `GET_ITEM` access.
